text: AWS S3 Public Bucket: Misconfiguration allowing public read/write. Detected via: AWS Config rule S3-BUCKET-PUBLIC-READ-PROHIBITED. Remediation: Block Public Access at account level, enable S3 Access Analyzer.
AWS IAM Least Privilege: Root account should not be used operationally. Use IAM roles with minimal permissions. Enable MFA on all IAM users. Use AWS Organizations SCPs to enforce guardrails.

Azure Active Directory: Enable Conditional Access. Block legacy authentication protocols. Use Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for just-in-time privileged access. Enable Identity Protection for risky sign-in detection.

GCP Security Best Practices: Enable VPC Service Controls. Use Workload Identity Federation instead of service account keys. Enable Cloud Audit Logs for all services. Use Binary Authorization for container image verification.
Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM): Tools: Prisma Cloud, AWS Security Hub, Azure Defender, GCP Security Command Center. Continuously assess cloud configurations against CIS Benchmarks.
Kubernetes RBAC: Avoid ClusterRole 'cluster-admin' for service accounts. Use namespace-scoped roles. Audit with: kubectl-who-can, rakkess, audit2rbac. Enable audit logging for API server.
Kubernetes Pod Security: Use Pod Security Admission (PSA) in 'restricted' mode. Prohibit: privileged containers, hostPath mounts, hostNetwork, runAsRoot. Enforce with OPA Gatekeeper or Kyverno.
Kubernetes Network Policies: Default deny all ingress/egress. Explicitly allow required communication paths. Use Calico or Cilium for L7 network policy enforcement.
Kubernetes Runtime Security: Deploy Falco for syscall-level anomaly detection. Monitor: container privilege escalation, unexpected outbound connections, file system writes to sensitive paths.
Container Image Security: Scan images with Trivy, Grype, or Snyk. Use distroless or scratch base images. Never use 'latest' tag in production. Sign images with Cosign (sigstore).
etcd Security: Enable TLS mutual authentication. Restrict access to etcd port (2379/2380). Encrypt etcd data at rest using KMS provider. Regular etcd backups — etcd contains ALL cluster secrets.

CIS Kubernetes Benchmark: 6 sections — Control Plane, Worker Nodes, Policies, Managed Services. Key controls: API server anonymous auth disabled, audit logging enabled, node restriction admission plugin.

Cloud Misconfiguration Statistics: 99% of cloud security failures will be customer's fault through 2025 (Gartner). Top misconfigs: overly permissive IAM, public storage, lack of encryption, disabled logging.

CloudTrail / Audit Logs: Always enable. Configure multi-region trail. Send to S3 with Object Lock (WORM). Alert on: root account usage, API calls from unexpected regions, IAM policy changes, security group changes.
